title: Pass 5.C.3: ferrotorch-jit AotCompiledModule + GraphSegment::Eager + InductorBackend silent fallbacks
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-jit audit, STUB bucket (3 findings).

- src/module.rs:344-355 — AotCompiledModule::forward_with_ctx docstring claims 'save intermediate tensors for backward', impl discards saved_tensor_indices and stores inputs.to_vec(). Self-admitting comment 'For now, save the inputs themselves'.
- src/graph_break.rs:487-500 — GraphSegment::Eager closure ignores its 'input' argument and returns the cached output_tensor snapshot from trace time. Comment lines 488-492: 'In a full implementation this would re-execute … For now, return it.' Reachable via pub trace_with_breaks / SegmentedModule::run.
- src/codegen.rs:1023 — InductorBackend::compile for CpuRust non-fusable path silently delegates to InterpreterBackend.compile(graph). 'Inductor as interpreter' for the long tail.

Acceptance:
- forward_with_ctx: implement real save-by-index from saved_tensor_indices, or fail-fast with clear error pointing at the unsupported case
- GraphSegment::Eager: re-execute the unsupported op against the actual input, or fail-fast
- InductorBackend: surface the fallback explicitly in the return type (not via silent delegation)
- Discriminating tests that catch the cached-snapshot regression for graph_break.
